_meta.title: The Psychedelic Nurse Clinical Research Companion — Seed Data
_meta.purpose: Internal facilitator reference for medication / supplement / street-drug compatibility with plant medicines
_meta.disclaimer: Harm-reduction reference only. Not medical advice. Every flagged interaction requires licensed clinician sign-off before ceremony.
sources: OHM Academy Pre-Ceremony Preparation Protocols (5-MeO-DMT, Psilocybin)
Global Ibogaine Therapy Alliance (GITA) Clinical Guidelines
Deeply Rooted Coaching — Bufo (5-MeO-DMT) Preparation & Integration Guide
Your Guide to a Safe 5-MeO-DMT Journey (Conclave / EntheoNation / Tandava)
Introduction to Iboga and Ibogaine — Taiboga Practitioner Notes

medicines.name: Iboga / Ibogaine
medicines.halfLife: Ibogaine ~7 hours; noribogaine stored in fat, weeks-long activity
medicines.metabolism: CYP2D6 — poor and ultra-rapid metabolizers at higher risk
highlightWarnings: QT prolongation and torsades de pointes risk
Bradycardia, hypotension, t-wave morphology changes (12–24h)
Adverse events reported as late as 76 hours post-dose
Lowers seizure threshold during alcohol/benzodiazepine withdrawal

medicines.name: Psilocybin
highlightWarnings: Lithium combination has caused seizures
Active psychosis or schizophrenia is an absolute contraindication
Modest BP and HR elevation

medicines.name: LSD
highlightWarnings: Lithium combination has caused seizures
Long duration amplifies psychiatric risk in vulnerable individuals
HPPD risk in predisposed individuals

medicines.name: MDMA
medicines.metabolism: CYP2D6 (also CYP3A4) — poor metabolizers at higher risk
highlightWarnings: Most cardiotoxic of common psychedelics
Serotonin syndrome risk with serotonergic drugs
Hyperthermia risk in warm environments / overexertion
Hyponatremia risk from over-hydration

medicines.name: 5-MeO-DMT
medicines.metabolism: MAO-A primarily; potentiated dramatically by MAOIs
highlightWarnings: Complete ego dissolution — psychological intensity is extreme
Cardiac and respiratory effects rapid in onset
MAOI combination is dangerous and prolongs experience
Lithium contraindicated

medicines.name: N,N-DMT
highlightWarnings: Without MAOI: short, intense, generally cardiovascular-mild
BP and HR elevation can be sharp
Smoked route — respiratory irritation

medicines.name: Ayahuasca
medicines.metabolism: Contains harmala alkaloids — reversible MAO-A inhibitors
highlightWarnings: Functions as an MAOI — tyramine-rich foods can trigger hypertensive crisis
SSRI combination risks serotonin syndrome
Tramadol, dextromethorphan, meperidine: can be fatal
Many medications metabolized by MAO-A become dangerous

medicines.name: Kambo
highlightWarnings: NOT a psychedelic — peptide medicine with intense purgative effects
Severe hyponatremia risk from forced water intake (deaths reported)
Cardiac contraindications: heart conditions, recent surgery, low BP
Pregnancy is an absolute contraindication

categories.name: Tricyclic Antidepressants
categories.description: Older-generation antidepressants with anticholinergic and serotonergic activity

categories.name: Methadone
categories.description: Long-acting opioid — special considerations for iboga

categories.name: Tramadol
categories.description: Opioid + SNRI activity — multiple serious interactions

categories.name: Dextromethorphan (DXM)
categories.description: Cough suppressant with serotonergic and NMDA activity

categories.name: Ondansetron (Zofran)
categories.description: Antiemetic — QT prolonging at higher doses

categories.name: Other Antihypertensives
categories.description: ACE inhibitors, ARBs, calcium channel blockers, diuretics

categories.name: Magnesium
categories.description: Mineral supplement — supports cardiac and nervous system function

categories.name: Grapefruit / CYP3A4 Inhibitors
categories.description: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors that affect drug metabolism

interactions.washout: 2–6 weeks (fluoxetine: 4–6 weeks due to long half-life)
interactions.restart: After QT and t-wave normalize on post-ceremony ECG (typically 24–72h+); coordinate with prescriber
interactions.notes: Both ibogaine and SSRIs prolong QT — combination risks torsades de pointes. SSRIs also blunt iboga's effect. Per GITA Clinical Guidelines, must wash out fully before flood dose. Coordinate taper with prescriber — abrupt SSRI discontinuation has its own risks.
interactions.source: Iboga Screening & Monitoring Brief; GITA Clinical Guidelines

interactions.restart: 24–48h post-ceremony if needed, coordinate with prescriber
interactions.notes: Chronic SSRI use significantly blunts psilocybin's subjective and therapeutic effects. Acute combination risk is low (serotonin syndrome rare) but the experience is often unproductive. Most clinical protocols (e.g., Compass, Usona) require taper. Discuss tradeoffs with participant and prescriber.
interactions.source: OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol; clinical research consensus

interactions.restart: 24–48h post-ceremony if needed, coordinate with prescriber
interactions.notes: Same logic as psilocybin — chronic SSRI use blunts LSD effects. Acute serotonin syndrome risk is low but not zero.
interactions.source: Tripsit harm reduction; clinical research consensus

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks (fluoxetine: 4–6 weeks). DO NOT proceed without full washout.
interactions.notes: DOUBLE PROBLEM: SSRIs block MDMA's mechanism (blunts effect, encourages re-dosing) AND combination raises serotonin syndrome risk. Hyperthermia risk amplified.
Per OHM 5-MeO-DMT Prep Protocol: SSRIs may require higher doses of 5-MeO-DMT (blunting effect) AND combination raises serotonin syndrome risk. Full medication disclosure mandatory.
interactions.source: OHM 5-MeO-DMT Prep Protocol; Conclave Best Practices

interactions.notes: Without MAOI: similar to psilocybin/LSD — blunted effect, low acute risk. With ayahuasca: see ayahuasca row.
Serotonin syndrome risk is real and has caused deaths. The MAOI activity of harmala alkaloids combined with SSRI is the classic dangerous combination. Full disclosure and washout mandatory.
interactions.source: Standard ayahuasca facilitator protocols; published case reports

interactions.washout: Generally not required for the peptides themselves
interactions.notes: Kambo peptides do not directly interact with serotonergic medications, but underlying psychiatric stability matters. Review with prescribing clinician.

interactions.restart: After QT/t-wave normalize, coordinate with prescriber
interactions.notes: Same QT and serotonergic concerns as SSRIs. Venlafaxine in particular has its own QT signal.
Same as SSRI. Norepinephrine component adds cardiovascular load.
Same serotonin syndrome logic as SSRI + ayahuasca.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks; 5 weeks for selegiline patch
interactions.notes: MAOIs are contraindicated with ibogaine.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks (4 weeks for selegiline patch)
interactions.notes: Hypertensive crisis and serotonin syndrome risk. Psilocybin's MAO-mediated breakdown also blocked — unpredictable potentiation.
Hypertensive crisis risk.
Has caused deaths. Hyperthermia + serotonin syndrome + hypertensive crisis. NEVER combine.
Per OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol: dramatically prolongs and intensifies effects, risk of serotonin syndrome.
Combining external DMT with prescription MAOI is essentially a non-traditional ayahuasca with additional pharmaceutical burden.
Compounding MAO inhibition. Hypertensive crisis risk. Tyramine reaction risk extreme.
No direct kambo-MAOI interaction known, but underlying psychiatric and cardiovascular status matters.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks; coordinate carefully with psychiatrist
interactions.notes: Lithium adds seizure risk and complicates cardiac picture.
interactions.source: GITA Clinical Guidelines; OHM Pre-Screening Questionnaire

interactions.notes: Lithium + classic psychedelic combination has well-documented seizure case reports. ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION per OHM Pre-Screening Questionnaire.
interactions.source: OHM Pre-Screening Questionnaire; published case reports

interactions.notes: Same as psilocybin — documented seizures. ABSOLUTE CONTRAINDICATION.
interactions.source: OHM Pre-Screening Questionnaire; published case reports

interactions.notes: Seizure risk and unpredictable interaction.
Seizure risk in combination with serotonergic psychedelic.
interactions.source: OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol; harm reduction consensus

interactions.notes: Seizure risk and serotonergic interaction.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks (longer for aripiprazole/Abilify due to long half-life)
interactions.notes: Many antipsychotics prolong QT — additive cardiac risk. Underlying psychiatric condition often itself contraindicated.
interactions.source: GITA Clinical Guidelines; OHM Pre-Screening Questionnaire

interactions.notes: Antipsychotics block 5-HT2A receptors — psilocybin's primary site. Effect is largely blocked, AND the underlying condition is typically a contraindication.
Same as psilocybin.
Blocks MDMA effects and underlying condition typically a contraindication.

interactions.washout: Minimum 1 week, longer for some agents
interactions.notes: Compounded cardiovascular load. Per Iboga Brief: stimulants/cocaine carry potentiation risk.
Compounded cardiovascular and hyperthermic load. Increased neurotoxicity risk.

interactions.washout: Hold day-of and 24h prior minimum; 1 week ideal
interactions.notes: Increases anxiety potential and cardiovascular load. Many participants tolerate but the experience is generally cleaner without.
interactions.source: OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol; harm reduction consensus

interactions.washout: MEDICAL TAPER REQUIRED before iboga — never abrupt discontinuation
interactions.notes: Per GITA: benzo dependence requires medical taper/stabilization before iboga/ibogaine. Withdrawal during ceremony lowers seizure threshold and can be lethal in combination with iboga.
interactions.source: Iboga Screening & Monitoring Brief; GITA Guidelines

interactions.washout: Hold day-of (12–24h prior); discuss chronic use
interactions.notes: Acute benzo strongly blunts/blocks psilocybin effect. Chronic use isn't dangerous but may reduce therapeutic value. Some facilitators keep emergency benzos available for severe acute distress.
Blunts experience. Chronic dependence may complicate the work.

interactions.washout: Transition long-acting opioids to short-acting 24–72h before; specific protocols required
interactions.restart: TOLERANCE IS RESET — relapse-dose risk of fatal overdose is extreme
interactions.notes: Iboga is used clinically TO INTERRUPT opioid dependence. This is one of the highest-risk transitions in medicine. Requires medical supervision. Iboga potentiates opioid effects acutely; afterward, opioid sensitivity returns to opioid-naive levels — same dose that was tolerated before iboga can kill after.
interactions.source: GITA Clinical Guidelines; Taiboga Practitioner Notes

interactions.washout: 24–72h depending on agent; tramadol and meperidine never combine
interactions.notes: Most opioids interact with MAOIs. Tramadol and meperidine have caused deaths.

interactions.washout: Transition to short-acting opioid 24–72h pre-dose. NEVER take iboga while still on methadone.
interactions.restart: Goal of treatment is often to NOT restart — but tolerance is reset, so any restart requires careful reduced dosing.
interactions.notes: Methadone has its own QT-prolonging signal — additive risk with iboga. Long half-life (~24-36h) means timing of transition is critical. This is specialist work.
interactions.source: Iboga Screening & Monitoring Brief; GITA Clinical Guidelines

interactions.restart: Avoid permanently if possible — multiple risks compound
interactions.notes: Tramadol has SNRI activity (serotonergic) AND opioid activity AND lowers seizure threshold. Triple problem with iboga.
Serotonin syndrome risk compounded.
Serotonin syndrome and seizure risk.
MAOI + tramadol can be fatal. One of the most dangerous combinations in psychedelic harm reduction.
interactions.source: Published case reports; standard ayahuasca protocols

interactions.notes: Seizure threshold lowering + serotonergic activity.
Seizure threshold lowering + serotonergic activity.
Serotonin syndrome risk. Check ALL cough/cold medicines.
Serotonin syndrome risk.
Serotonin syndrome risk.
Serotonergic + cardiac concerns.
Per Iboga Brief: QT-prolonging agents must wash out. Fluoroquinolones (Cipro, Levaquin, etc.) prolong QT independently — additive risk dangerous.
Z-pak and other macrolides prolong QT.

interactions.washout: Avoid in pre-ceremony period; if antiemetic needed, prefer metoclopramide or promethazine
interactions.notes: Per Iboga Brief: high-dose ondansetron prolongs QT.
Per Iboga Brief: CYP interactions affect ibogaine metabolism. Also serotonergic activity.
Serotonergic activity + MAOI = serotonin syndrome risk.
Serotonin syndrome risk.
Serotonin syndrome risk.

interactions.restart: Can be helpful AFTER MDMA for serotonin replenishment, but not with ayahuasca
interactions.notes: Direct serotonin precursor + MAOI = serotonin syndrome risk.

interactions.restart: Often helpful 1–3 days post-MDMA for serotonin restoration (not within 24h)
interactions.notes: Pre-MDMA: serotonin syndrome risk. Post-MDMA: commonly used in harm-reduction protocols for restoration, but timing matters.
Serotonin loading risk.
Per Iboga Brief: target Mg²⁺ ≥2.0 pre-dose. Magnesium is repleted IV before iboga in clinical settings. Oral supplementation pre-ceremony is supportive.
Per OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol: magnesium glycinate or threonate 200–400 mg daily recommended in prep week; 300–600 mg split doses days 2–1 prior.
Per OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol: 200–400 mg daily in prep week; supports nervous system regulation.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks; full taper may require longer for chronic users
interactions.restart: Goal is often abstinence — restart resets opioid tolerance complications
interactions.notes: Kratom has opioid receptor activity. Combined with iboga — same overdose-on-restart risk as other opioids. Often unreported by participants because considered 'herbal.' ASK directly.
Some serotonergic activity + MAOI. Not as well-characterized as classic opioids but caution warranted.
Per Iboga Brief: grapefruit, licorice root, St. John's Wort all have CYP interactions affecting ibogaine metabolism.

interactions.washout: Minimum 72h for occasional use; MEDICAL TAPER REQUIRED for dependence
interactions.restart: Minimum 1 week post-ceremony; consider longer for integration
interactions.notes: Per GITA: alcohol dependence requires medical taper before iboga. Withdrawal during ceremony lowers seizure threshold significantly. Iboga itself doesn't cause seizures, but in withdrawal context the combination can be lethal.
interactions.source: Iboga Screening & Monitoring Brief; GITA Clinical Guidelines

interactions.restart: Minimum 24h post; ideally longer for integration
interactions.notes: Per OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol: minimum 72 hours prior. Heavy/dependent use requires assessment.

interactions.washout: Minimum 72h prior; ideally 30 days for full clearing
interactions.notes: Per OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol and Conclave Best Practices: 30 days prior optimal; 72h minimum.
interactions.source: OHM 5-MeO-DMT Prep Protocol; Conclave Best Practices

interactions.washout: Minimum 1 week prior; longer for traditional dieta
interactions.restart: Minimum 1 week post; traditional dietas extend much longer
interactions.notes: Traditional ayahuasca dieta restricts alcohol significantly. Tyramine in some alcoholic beverages (esp. aged wine, beer) compounds MAOI risk.

interactions.washout: Minimum 72h; ideally 1–2 weeks for chronic users
interactions.restart: Avoid during integration window (minimum 2–4 weeks post)
interactions.notes: Cannabis can interact unpredictably with iboga's psychological effects. Chronic use also affects CYP metabolism.
Per OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol: avoid recreational substances. Cannabis can amplify anxiety and unpredictability during ceremony, and during integration can blunt insights.
Per OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol: 'Recreational substances of any kind' avoided 72h+. 30 days for full clearing per Tandava.

interactions.washout: Minimum 1 week prior; traditional dieta longer
interactions.notes: Traditional ayahuasca dieta excludes cannabis. Can amplify nausea and psychological intensity unpredictably.
CBD can blunt psilocybin's effects. CBD also inhibits CYP enzymes — affects metabolism of other meds.
May blunt effects; CYP interactions.

interactions.washout: Minimum 1 week, longer for chronic use; medical clearance
interactions.notes: Per Iboga Brief: stimulants/cocaine carry potentiation risk. Cardiotoxicity is the major concern.
Compounded cardiotoxicity and hyperthermia.
Cardiac risk compounded; psychological destabilization.

interactions.washout: Minimum 2 weeks; medical clearance for chronic use
interactions.notes: Cardiotoxicity. Withdrawal also a complicating factor for the iboga experience itself.
Recent MDMA depletes serotonin. Subsequent psychedelic within ~6 weeks may be muted or dysphoric.
Same as psilocybin.
Serotonin depletion + cardiac concerns.
Serotonin depletion + MAOI = unpredictable.
No direct interaction but separating experiences allows integration.
Adds cardiovascular load. Withdrawal during ceremony adds discomfort.
Per Conclave Best Practices: nicotine can disrupt mental and physical grounding.
Adds cardiovascular load. Withdrawal headaches during ceremony complicate the picture.

interactions.washout: Reduce in prep week; avoid day-of
interactions.notes: Per OHM Psilocybin Prep Protocol: 'Reduce or eliminate alcohol and caffeine' in prep week. Can amplify anxiety.
Per OHM 5-MeO Prep Protocol: 'all stimulants: caffeine, pre-workouts, energy drinks' avoided.
Per Iboga Brief: TCAs are QT-prolonging. Additive cardiac risk.
Serotonergic activity + MAOI.
Serotonergic and cardiac risk.

interactions.washout: Discuss with prescriber; some require 2 weeks
interactions.notes: Underlying condition (often bipolar) requires careful evaluation. Per OHM Pre-Screening: bipolar requires medical clearance.
Bipolar diagnosis requires high-risk medical clearance per OHM Pre-Screening.

interactions.washout: Discuss with cardiologist — may need to hold or substitute
interactions.notes: Iboga causes bradycardia. Beta blockers compound bradycardia and AV block risk.
Beta blockers blunt MDMA's stimulant effects (somewhat protective for HR/BP) but underlying cardiac condition remains a concern.

interactions.washout: Discuss with prescriber — iboga causes hypotension, additive risk
interactions.notes: Iboga causes hypotension. Existing antihypertensives compound this. Underlying hypertension also a flag.
Underlying hypertension is itself a concern; MDMA acutely raises BP.

all_medicines: Schizophrenia or any psychotic disorder diagnosis
Active suicidal ideation with a plan
Currently taking MAOIs (with most substances — see specifics)
Currently taking antipsychotic medication (must wash out)
iboga_additional: QTc >450 ms (men) / >470 ms (women) on baseline ECG (some clinics use 430/450)
History of long QT syndrome, structural heart disease, recent MI
Active alcohol or benzodiazepine dependence without medical taper

high_risk_requires_clearance: Severe lasting distress from prior psychedelic use
